item.title: Efficacy and safety of freshly collected autologous adipose tissue for complex anal fistulas in non-IBD patients: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Complex cryptoglandular anal fistulas present a treatment challenge, with many surgical options associated with recurrence, variable healing rates, and risk of incontinence. Freshly collected autol...

item.title: Prevalence of inflammatory bowel disease across states: estimates based on claims database.
item.description: Inflammatory bowel diseases - Data derived from our previously published pooled administrative claims database analysis were applied to estimate race-weighted state-specific prevalences of inflammatory bowel diseases in the Uni...

item.title: Diagnostic delays and persistent symptoms in lymphogranuloma venereum among men who have sex with men in Poland: a single-centre case series.
item.description: BMC infectious diseases - Lymphogranuloma venereum (LGV) caused by Chlamydia trachomatis serovars L1-L3 is increasingly reported among men who have sex with men (MSM) in Western Europe, yet appears to remain underrecognised...

item.title: Determinants of influenza vaccination uptake in older adults: findings from a retrospective cross-sectional study.
item.description: BMC public health - Influenza poses a serious health risk to older adults, and although annual vaccination is the most effective prevention, coverage in this population remains inconsistent. This study assessed influe...

item.title: Scrotal abscesses in perianal Crohn's disease: a retrospective cohort study of clinical presentation, management, and outcomes.
item.description: Techniques in coloproctology - Perianal Crohn's disease (pCD) is a severe phenotype frequently complicated by fistulas and recurrent abscesses. Although perianal sepsis is well described, genital involvement is rare, with scrota...

item.title: Incidence and prevalence of inflammatory Bowel disease in China: a regional population-based study from 2020 to 2022.
item.description: Scandinavian journal of gastroenterology - The global burden of inflammatory bowel diseases (IBDs), comprising Crohn's disease (CD) and ulcerative colitis (UC), is on the rise. Studies regarding the incidence of IBD in China remain notably ...

item.title: Incidence and risk factors of rebleeding in Crohn's disease patients with acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding.
item.description: The Korean journal of internal medicine - Multicenter study - Acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding (LGIB) in Crohn's disease (CD) is a rare but potentially life-threatening complication. This study aimed to investigate the incidence and risk factors of reble...

item.title: Trends and disparities in the burden of autoimmune diseases in China and globally: A comparative analysis based on the Global Burden of Disease Study 2021.
item.description: Medicine - This study aimed to quantify long-term trends and burdens of 5 autoimmune diseases in China and globally from 1990 to 2021 using Global Burden of Disease 2021 data and identify drivers of change to...

item.title: Early post-operative outcomes in adults undergoing minimally invasive versus open emergency colorectal surgery: A retrospective cohort study of the ACS-NSQIP 2016-2022.
item.description: Colorectal disease : the official journal of the Association of Coloproctology of Great Britain and Ireland - Minimally invasive surgery (MIS) has become common practice in elective colectomy; however, in emergent settings, this is less common and requires further research on safety and efficacy. The aim o...

item.title: Crohn's Disease Activity Index in the era of obesity: time to abandon it?
item.description: Journal of Crohn's & colitis - Obesity and metabolic syndrome are now common among patients with Crohn's disease, with prevalence rates approaching those of the general population. Yet the Crohn's Disease Activity Index (CDAI), ...

item.title: Association between energy-adjusted dietary inflammatory index and increased risk of all-cause Crohn's disease: a prospective cohort study.
item.description: Frontiers in public health - Crohn's disease (CD) is a chronic inflammatory bowel condition with increasing global incidence. Diet is a key modulator of chronic inflammation, often assessed by the Dietary Inflammatory Index (D...

item.title: Ischemic colitis in a child: a case report and narrative review.
item.description: Frontiers in pediatrics - Case report - Ischemic colitis is extremely rare in preschool-aged children and is still susceptible to misdiagnosis due to atypical symptoms. Misdiagnosis can have serious consequences, including delayed treatm...
